hero.question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?
hero.prob_take: The crowd has essentially decided: 94.8% odds means bettors are pricing in a rates hold at April 2026 as near-certain, implying either a terminal rate well before then or economic conditions that don't justify a move. The real question is whether the Fed actually signals rate cuts before April or whether inflation data forces recalibration, which would crater this position fast if consensus shifts on cuts.

daily_take.headline: Fed Holds Steady in April 2026. Market Pricing It at 95%. That's Lazy.
daily_take.deck: The Fed pause trade just jumped 2 points to 94.8%, riding soft inflation data and hawkish pivot exhaustion. But this assumes Powell keeps his hands in his pockets for 15 months straight. If Q1 2026 data shows any heat, this collapses. Sharp bettors are laying this at 90 cents or lower and waiting for the economic data to do the talking.
